properties.ParkDesc: A place for the whole family to gather. Let your kids play on the pump track with their bikes, or enjoy a soccer game on the smaller scale field. Features an imaginative and whimsical play structure, made from tree trunks and roots.
properties.LocDesc: In Blaeberry, approximately 9.5 kms north of Golden, turn right (east) off Hwy 1 onto Moberly Branch Rd, then left onto Golden Upper Donald Rd, and right onto Oberg Johnson Rd. Park is on the left.

properties.ParkDesc: Rugged, forested bench above the Columbia River. Small, pet-friendly campground and day-use areas. Lakeside picnic areas, vault toilet, and boat-launch. Provides access to the Moonraker Trail System.
properties.LocDesc: Southwest of Golden, travel along  Kicking Horse Trail to Dogtooth FSR. Campground access is approximately 1.5 km along Dogtooth FSR, and then 0.5 km along Cedar Lake FSR.

properties.ParkDesc: This is the highest elevation of all CSRD Parks and it includes picnic tables and benches to take in the spectacular views, or to enjoy a picnic. There is a vault toilet and a playground, as well as a grassy, free play area.
properties.LocDesc: West of Golden, in Kicking Horse, just off Kicking Horse Trail.

properties.ParkDesc: Trailhead for CBT trails offering parking, washrooms, waste disposal and an information kiosk. Trails are managed and maintained by the Golden Cycling Club.
properties.LocDesc: Just west of Golden off 10 Ave/Hwy 95, approximately 2.75 km up Kicking Horse Drive.

properties.ParkDesc: Parson Community Recreation Park is a year-round recreation park with a skating and sledding in the winter, and basketball, beach volleyball, and tennis courts in the summer. Also has a warming hut, playground, picnic shelter, and vault toilets.
properties.LocDesc: In Parson, approximately 33 km south of Golden on Highway 95.

properties.ParkDesc: This park is adjacent to the Begbie Falls and Mt Macpherson XcTr Recreation Areas and incldues 1 km of rugged shoreline along Upper Arrow Lake and part of the trail Sex on the Beach.
properties.LocDesc: South of Revelstoke off Hwy 23. From the Hwy turn onto Mt Begbie Rd (or Clough Rd) to access Begbie Falls FSR. Birchcliffe Park is on the lake side of the road.

properties.ParkDesc: Nestled in a beautiful forested space, this park features looping bike skills trails, perfect for beginners, and a gentle walking trail to a spectacular vista overlooking Copper Island on Shuswap Lake. Benches, toilets, and a small parking area.
properties.LocDesc: In Blind Bay, turn north off Hwy 1 onto Golf Course drive and follow it west until it becomes Cedar Dr. Turn right off Cedar Drive onto Mt Tuam Crescent and look for the park on the left.

properties.ParkDesc: A popular Shuswap Lake beach for deep water swimming with a partially shaded, small-stone shore. Washroom facilities located nearby at the Blind Bay Community Hall.
properties.LocDesc: In Blind Bay, this beach is located just north of Sunrise Blvd and Blind Bay Rd intersection, on the shore of Shuswap Lake.

properties.ParkDesc: A popular Shuswap Lake beach for swimming with a partially shaded, sandy shore. Picnic tables onsite, and washroom facilities located nearby at the Blind Bay Community Hall.
properties.LocDesc: In Blind Bay, this beach is located just south of Marine Drive and Blind Bay Rd intersection, on the shore of Shuswap Lake.

properties.ParkDesc: Public lake access via gravel pathway with planted native species on either side. At the lakeshore there is an artisan's bench, a beautiful place for relaxing and taking in breathtaking views.
properties.LocDesc: In Blind Bay, just off Blind Bay Rd, approximately 0.8 km west of the intersection with Sunrise Blvd, on the shore of Shuswap Lake.

properties.ParkDesc: Large park includes playground with sun-shade, ball diamond, and field. Vault toilet, benches, and picnic tables.
properties.LocDesc: In Blind Bay, nestled in between Ta-Lana Trail and Lakeview Drive.

properties.ParkDesc: A great place to enjoy a picnic at the lake. Amenities include a vault toilet, picnic benches, parking, and a wheelchair-friendly trail.
properties.LocDesc: Located approximately 3 km east of Paradise Point, east off Hwy 1 at Sunnybrae, approximately 17.7 km along Sunnybrae-Canoe Pt Rd, on the shore of Shuswap Lake.

properties.ParkDesc: A great intermediate trail for an afternoon walk (uphill) through lovely mixed forests to a spectacular little lookout over the lake.
properties.LocDesc: In Sorrento, south of Hwy 1. Exit highway onto Elson Rd from the east or Defosses Rd from the west. Trailhead is off South Shore Pl.

properties.ParkDesc: Large park with over 400 metres of rocky shoreline on Shuswap Lake. Parking opposite beach (across road) with vault toilet. Features a wheelchair-friendly picnic area with beautiful views of the lake, as well as a designated swim area with platform.
properties.LocDesc: In Eagle Bay, approximately 19 kms along Eagle Bay Rd (from intersection with Balmoral Rd). On the shore of Shuswap Lake.

properties.ParkDesc: Gail Road Waterfront Park is a lake access in the Eagle Bay area. It provides picnic tables, garbage and recycling receptacles, a vault toilet and parking for three vehicles.
properties.LocDesc: In Eagle Bay , from Eagle Bay Rd, turn north onto Bravo Rd and then east onto Parker Rd.

properties.ParkDesc: This park includes a short recreational trail with a few historical points of interest.
properties.LocDesc: In Eagle Bay, approximately 17.5 kms along Eagle Bay Rd (from intersection with Balmoral Rd). On the shore of Shuswap Lake.

properties.ParkDesc: Day-use park with a hand-crafted wooden train viewing platform. The park shares the site with Notch Hill Hall, one of the oldest community halls in the Shuswap dating back to 1910, as well as the Notch Hill School (1921) and Notch Hill Church (1906).
properties.LocDesc: In Notch Hill, on Notch Hill Rd, approximately 4 km south off Hwy 1 in Sorrento.

properties.ParkDesc: Rocky Point Road is a small lake access on the end of a cul-de-sac. It has picturesque views of Blind Bay, Copper Island and Shuswap Lake.
properties.LocDesc: In Reedman Point, turn west off Eagle Bay Rd onto Rocky Pt Rd and follow it approximately 0.5 km to its terminus on the shore of Shuswap Lake.

properties.ParkDesc: North-west access to the Blind Bay White Lake trail system. Parking for 10-15 vehicles, a vault toilet and garbage receptacle for public convenience. McArthur Heights trail is steep and difficult, leading to a magnificent viewpoint.
properties.LocDesc: In Reedman Point, off Eagle Bay Rd. Turn east onto Reedman Pt Rd and then follow McBride Rd to it's terminus.

properties.ParkDesc: A great spot for family recreation includes a playground, ample parking, washrooms, a picnic area and beachfront on Shuswap Lake. There is a short trail up to the top of the bluffs across the road for a wonderful view of the lake.
properties.LocDesc: In Sunnybrae, east off Hwy 1, approximately 3.5 km along Sunnybrae-Canoe Pt Rd. Parking area is on the right, on the shore of Shuswap Lake.

properties.ParkDesc: The Reinecker Creek Trails begin in Herald Provincial Park and continue north onto crown land on the east and west sides of Reinecker Creek.
properties.LocDesc: In Sunnybrae, east off Hwy 1, approximately 12 km along Sunnybrae-Canoe Pt Rd. Park in the Margaret Falls parking or at Herald Park.

properties.ParkDesc: Loftus Park is a trail network built to universal trail standards (wheelchair accessible). The parking lot has parking for 14 vehicles and has bus parking. There is a 1355 m2 dog park area, accessible vault toilet, picnic tables and benches.
properties.LocDesc: In Blind Bay, turn north off Hwy 1 at Balmoral Rd. Follow Balmoral Rd for approximately 1 km and turn left onto Golf Course Drive. Fox Glen Way is the first road on the right and the park access is at the end of the road.

properties.ParkDesc: Cement boat launch with a 60-ft dock for loading/unloading. Vault toilet and garbage receptacles on-site. No moorage or overnight parking is permitted.
properties.LocDesc: In Blind Bay, just off Eagle Bay Rd on the shore of Shuswap Lake.

properties.ParkDesc: Gillespie Road Community Park boasts scenic views, a swimming dock, a picnic area, a vault toilet and garbage receptacles.  This beautiful spot is a perfect getaway in the summer for recreation.
properties.LocDesc: In Sorrento (East), north off Blind Bay Rd, at the end of Gillespie Rd. On the shore of Shuswap Lake.

properties.ParkDesc: Includes a perimeter trail, batting cage, tennis courts, soccer field, basketball court, 3 baseball diamonds with beautiful viewing areas, concession stands, dog park, water spray park, and a playground with a state of the art sun shade.
properties.LocDesc: In Sorrento, turn south off Hwy 1 onto Notch Hill Rd. Then turn right onto Dilworth Rd and left onto Davidson Rd. The park is on the left (east) side of the road.

properties.ParkDesc: Caen Road Park is a grassy park located in Blind Bay.  Used as a day use park and swimming area, Caen Road Park is a beautiful destination for swimming and picnicking. This is also a hand-launch site for boats.
properties.LocDesc: In Sorrento, north off Hwy 1, approximately 0.5 km down Caen Rd, on the shore of Shuswap Lake.

properties.ParkDesc: Year round boat launch in Sorrento with parking for 11 vehicles and trailers. There is a vault toilet, a garbage receptacle, and a boat dock for temporary moorage while loading and unloading. Overnight parking is not permitted.
properties.LocDesc: In Sorrento, north off Hwy 1, approximately 0.5 km to the end of Caen Rd, on the shore of Shuswap Lake.

properties.ParkDesc: South-west access to the Blind Bay White Lake trail system.  Ample parking, vault toilet.  Superb hiking and mountain biking opportunities.
properties.LocDesc: In Blind Bay, turn north off Hwy 1 at Balmoral Rd. Follow Balmoral Rd for approximately 1.5 km down Balmoral Rd.

properties.ParkDesc: This park is an excellent, multi-difficulty bike skills park located in White Lake. Ammenities include ample parking, a playground, pincic area, and washrooms. This is also the east trailhead for the the Blind Bay White Lake trail system.
properties.LocDesc: In White Lake, approxmately 0.5 km west of the firehall and community centre.

properties.ParkDesc: This is a wonderful all season family park boasting excellent winter ice fishing. Includes a vault toilet, picnic area, and viewing platform. As well as a boat launch and fixed dock. Parking for vehicles and trailers across road adjacent to fire hall.
properties.LocDesc: In White Lake, on Parri Rd, across from the firehall and community centre. On the shore of White Lake.

properties.ParkDesc: Large park with 70 metres of shoreline on White Lake. Parking for 6 vehicles, washroom, picnic benches, and a trail that leads down a steep slope to the beach area. Excellent, roped off swim area with platform.
properties.LocDesc: In White Lake, on Parri Rd, approximately 1 km east of the firehall and community centre. On the shore of White Lake.

properties.ParkDesc: Provincial Recreation Site encompassing the Blind Bay White Lake Trail system
properties.LocDesc: Access this system from one of 3 trailheads: MacArthur Heights (Reedman Point), Balmoral (Blind Bay), or John Evdokimoff (White Lake).

properties.ParkDesc: Secluded and rarely visited park (due to distance from highway) with unsurpassed views across Shuswap Lake. Amenities include ample parking, washrooms, and a hiking trail from the parking lot to the shoreline.
properties.LocDesc: At Wild Rose Bay, approximately 26 kms along Eagle Bay Rd (from intersection with Balmoral Rd). On the shore of Shuswap Lake.

properties.ParkDesc: Year-round boat launch with vault toilet, six parking stalls and a dock for loading/unloading. No overnight parking or moorage is permitted.
properties.LocDesc: In Eagle Bay, approximately 23 kms along Eagle Bay Rd (from intersection with Balmoral Rd). On the shore of Shuswap Lake.

properties.ParkDesc: Lake access with hand-launch for small boats on Gardom Lake. Gas-powered motors are not permitted on the lake. Site includes a vault toilet, picnic table, and ample parking.
properties.LocDesc: In Deep Creek, turn west off Hwy 97B onto Deep Creek Rd. Then, left onto Mallory Rd. After approximately 3.7 km, turn left onto Musgrave Rd and follow to it's terminus, on the shore of Gardom Lake.

properties.ParkDesc: Bolean Creek Community Park is a small community park located in Falkland near the Falkland Rodeo Grounds. This site has a washroom and 3 picnic tables (one handicap accessible table) in a small grassy area. Parking for 6 cars.
properties.LocDesc: In Falkland, just east of the Rodeo Grounds, off Hwy 97.

properties.ParkDesc: Newly updated multi-use trail system within the Glenemma Rec Reserve. Toilet, picnic table, and lots of parking including trailer pull-through and staging areas.
properties.LocDesc: In Glenemma, west off Salmon River Rd. Access via Yankee Flats Rd, McTavish Rd, and Kelly Main FSR.

properties.ParkDesc: Popular fishing destination with a boat launch and day-use parking, as well as 15 first-come-first-serve, fee-free camp sites.
properties.LocDesc: This is a MOTSA (Forestry) Rec Site located 9 km north of Falkland on the Chase Falkland Road

properties.ParkDesc: The west side of this large park has beautiful old growth Ponderosa Pine and Douglas Fir stands. The east portion contains a baseball field, concession stand, and toilets. A horse riding ring expands this day use park into a multi-purpose facility.
properties.LocDesc: In Silver Creek, off Salmon River Rd. Main park access is just north of the firehall.

properties.ParkDesc: Campground operated by the CSRD within the Yard Creek Provincial Park. 60 hectares of beautiful forested uplands and 115 hectares of riparian habitat.
properties.LocDesc: In Malakwa, approximately 15 km east of Sicamous, east off the Trans Canada Hwy. Exit the highway onto Oxbow Frtg Rd, and then turn onto Yard Creek Loop Rd. Campground is approximately 0.75 km up the road.

properties.ParkDesc: A spectacular park bordering the North Fork of the Perry River. An impressive network of trails that wind through the land's intact primary cedar and hemlock forests. Taking you to beaches, creeks, sloughs, and the inspiring Lower Perry River Canyon.
properties.LocDesc: Approximately 5 km north of Malakwa, turn north-west off Hwy 1 onto Avoca Rd West. Follow for approximately 1 km to the lower parking area.

properties.ParkDesc: This trail is an easy 0.5 km hike through rich hemlock with the constant soothing, serenade of Sicamous Creek around you.
properties.LocDesc: Approximately 1 km south of Sicamous, turn east off Hwy 97A onto Two Mile Rd, towards the landfill. The trailhead is approximately 0.5 km up the road on the right.

properties.ParkDesc: Lakefront swimming and picnic area in Anglemont. There is over 200 metres of south-facing beach, with a picnic area, vault toilet, garbage receptacles and recycling containers for public convenience.
properties.LocDesc: In Anglemont, just off Squilax-Anglemont Rd.

properties.ParkDesc: Parking and access to the Evelyn Falls hiking trail.
properties.LocDesc: In Anglemont, at at the north end of Golf Course Rd.

properties.ParkDesc: St Ives Community Park has been recently upgraded and includes a new retaining wall, a vault toilet, garbage and recycling bins, four new picnic tables, landscaping, a fire ring and new signage. It also has a swimming area and sandy beach.
properties.LocDesc: In St Ives, approximately 0.7 km east of Horseshoe Bay, off Squilax-Anglemont Rd. Turn right onto Southwind Rd and the park will be at the corner of Southwind Rd and St Ives Way. On the shore of Shuswap Lake.

properties.ParkDesc: Pedestrian access to Shuswap Lake through a narrow, treed road allowance.
properties.LocDesc: In Anglemont, approximately 1.5 kms east of the mouth of Ross Creek. Turn right off Squilax-Anglemont Rd onto Welch Rd, and follow to its terminus on the shore of Shuswap Lake.

properties.ParkName: Farrell's Field Community Park / Ice Rink
properties.ParkDesc: All season park known for its outdoor ice rink. Amenities include a heated skate shack, flood lights and a sound system. Goal nets are available. Folks can enjoy a trail network to explore the park and to make their way down to the canyon at Hlina Creek.
properties.LocDesc: In Celista, approximately 2 km up Meadow Crek Rd.

properties.ParkDesc: This waterfront park includes a vault toilet, picnic table and grassed picnic area, large parking lot for truck and trailer parking, Kids Dont Float kiosk with life jackets for the public, a dock and boat launch. The boat launch is open April 15-Sept 30.
properties.LocDesc: In Celista, approximately 0.75 km west of Celista Park, off Squilax-Anglemont Rd, on the shore of Shuswap Lake.

properties.ParkDesc: A fantastic park on Shuswap Lake with scenic views and beautiful grounds with a mix of shade and sun. A gently sloping packed gravel trail allows the best viewpoints to be accessible by everyone. Ample parking, a vault toilet, and picnic tables.
properties.LocDesc: In Lee Creek, just off Squilax-Anglemont Rd, on the shore of Shuswap Lake.

properties.ParkDesc: Popular swimming and picnic area with over 200 metres of south facing beach, a picnic area, and a vault toilet. Parking is available for 10 - 12 cars.  To the west of the beach, Ross Creek feeds into the lake.  Explore and enjoy this beautiful park.
properties.LocDesc: In Magna Bay, at the mouth of Ross Creek, on Squilax-Anglemont Rd.

properties.ParkDesc: Popular swimming and picnic area with over 100 metres of south facing, fine gravel beach. Vault toilet and public boat launch with loading dock. No overnight parking or moorage.
properties.LocDesc: In Magna Bay, at the intersection of Squilax-Anglemont Rd and Line 17 Rd, on the shore of Shuswap Lake.

properties.ParkDesc: This park is the recreational heart of Scotch Creek and the North Shuswap. The 4.5 acre park includes a playground, baseball diamond and ball field, all-court (tennis, basketball, ball hockey), swings, picnic area and flush toilet.
properties.LocDesc: In Scotch Creek on Butters Rd just off Squilax-Anglemont Rd. Adjacent to Imai Ball Park.

properties.ParkDesc: A popular destination in the summer months for locals and visitors alike. There are over 100 metres of south facing beach, with a picnic area, and vault toilet. Parking is available for up to eight vehicles.
properties.LocDesc: In Scotch Creek, turn south off Squilax-Anglemont Rd onto Scotch Creek Wharf Rd and follow it approx 1.3 km to it's terminus on Shuswap Lake. Alternatively, park at Shuswap Lake Park and use the 3 km parallel trail to travel to Scotch Creek Wharf Rd Park.

properties.ParkDesc: A forest walk to impressive views over Shuswap Lake high above Scotch Creek.
properties.LocDesc: In Scotch Creek, from Squilax-Anglemont Rd, turn north-west onto Leopold Rd. After approximately 2.5 km, turn north up Scotch Creek Hlina FSR for approximately 4.5 km.

properties.ParkDesc: Lake front swimming and picnic area. Includes vault toilet, fire rings, and parking for 12-15 vehicles.
properties.LocDesc: In Seymour Arm, just off Daniels Rd on the shore of Shuswap Lake.

properties.ParkDesc: This community park is a great choice for family or group gatherings.  With a ball field, swing set, horse-shoe pitch and volleyball area, the recreational opportunities are abundant. Parking, picnic area, and toilets.
properties.LocDesc: In Seymour Arm, at Abbott Rd and Collings Rd.

properties.ParkDesc: Malakwa Community Park features a playground, a bounder nature-play area, outdoor fitness equipment, a timber-frame picnic shelter, and a community garden. There is also a large grassy area and short trails.
properties.LocDesc: In Malakwa, north-west off Hwy 1. Use Malakwa Rd to access Community Hall Rd.

properties.ParkDesc: This accessible community park has recycled rubber surfacing, a variety of play features including accessible playground equipment, a multi use sports court, picnic shelter, accessible vault toilet, nature play, and outdoor exercise equipment for seniors
properties.LocDesc: In Eagle Bay, parking and access at Eagle Bay Community Centre.

properties.ParkDesc: Shady lakefront park that includes multiple play structures, picnic tables, washrooms, and access to Mara Lake.
properties.LocDesc: At Swansea Point, west off Hwy 97A, at the end of Swanson Rd, on the shore of Mara Lake.

properties.ParkDesc: This lake access includes a sandy beach with beach volleyball court, picnic tables, and parking for up to three vehicles.
properties.LocDesc: At Swansea Point, west off Hwy 97A, just off Swanbeach Rd, on the shore of Mara Lake.

properties.ParkDesc: A picturesque lake access with picnic benches and a bike rack. Please be aware, there are no public washrooms here. The closest washroom is at Cambridge Road Lake Access.
properties.LocDesc: At Swansea Point, west off Hwy 97A, just off Swanshore Rd, on the shore of Mara Lake.

properties.ParkDesc: This grassy park is a great place to bring your family to spend the day outside. Amenities include a bike rack, vault toilet, four picnic tables including one that is handicap accessible, and parking for up to six vehicles.
properties.LocDesc: At Swansea Point, west off Hwy 97A, just off Swanbeach Rd, on the shore of Mara Lake.

properties.ParkDesc: Day use public boat launch and loading dock (yield to emergency vehicles accessing the lake).  Includes a bike rack, parking and picnic tables. Please be aware, there are no public washrooms on site. The closest washroom is at Cambridge Road Lake Access.
properties.LocDesc: At Swansea Point, west off Hwy 97A, just off Swanshore Rd, on the shore of Mara Lake.

properties.ParkDesc: Oxford Road in Swansea Point is a lake access park on Mara Lake, great for hand launching boats and swimming. On site at Oxford Road, you will find a washroom, bike rack and parking for up to four vehicles.
properties.LocDesc: At Swansea Point, west off Hwy 97A, just off Swanshore Rd, on the shore of Mara Lake.

properties.ParkDesc: East access to the Blind Bay White Lake trail system. Park at the bike park. Vault toilets, picnic area, and playground at this location.
properties.LocDesc: In White Lake, approxmately 0.5 km west of the firehall and community centre.
